notes_md:
**mái tóc** means “hair” on someone’s head, usually talking about the whole head of hair. It feels a bit more descriptive or poetic than just **tóc**.

It’s a noun. You can say **một mái tóc**, but in daily use people often just say **tóc** without a classifier.  

Common pairings: **mái tóc dài/ngắn/đen/vàng/xoăn/thẳng**, and verbs like **cắt tóc, nhuộm tóc, buộc tóc**.  

Think of **mái tóc** as “a head of hair” — the full look, not just individual hairs.
exampleSentencesTranslated: She really likes the color of her new hair.
This morning he spent time brushing his hair neatly.
I want to cut my hair shorter for this summer.
exampleSentences: Chị rất thích màu mái tóc mới của mình.
Sáng nay anh dành thời gian chải mái tóc thật gọn gàng.
Em muốn cắt mái tóc ngắn hơn cho mùa hè này.

Common pairings: mái tóc dài/ngắn/đen/vàng/xoăn/thẳng, and verbs like cắt tóc, nhuộm tóc, buộc tóc.  

Think of mái tóc as “a head of hair” — the full look, not just individual hairs.
